title: Prototype record: starter lifecycle trace review
objective: Verify that a new product team can understand the lifecycle trace model from a detailed interactive starter example.
uncertainty_statement: It is still unclear whether a high-detail interactive prototype will reduce onboarding ambiguity more effectively than a static seeded doc example.
primary_test_scenario: A PM reviews the seeded lifecycle item, opens stage evidence, and decides whether the discovery package is ready for PRD handoff.
experience_quality_target: The prototype should feel polished and realistic enough to support a serious workflow walkthrough rather than a narrated concept pitch.
tested_assumptions: A seeded example reduces onboarding ambiguity
Teams can understand the lifecycle model without reconstructing the story manually
proof_generated: A concrete example improves startup comprehension of the ProductOS trace model
quality_reference_examples: A product walkthrough detailed enough for PM review without live narration
learnings: A seeded interactive example reduces ambiguity during workspace setup.
Visible gate evidence makes the prototype feel closer to a credible v1 surface than a static explanation.
improvement_actions: Add richer interaction around stage transitions and artifact previews in the next prototype pass.
Tighten visual hierarchy so current state, proof, and deferred work are distinguishable at a glance.
strategic_implication: The prototype supports a niche posture based on concrete lifecycle clarity rather than generic starter breadth.
